title: Lifecycle, Storage Classes, Archive Restore, and Cost · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds lifecycle and storage-cost decision table, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of lifecycle and storage-cost decision table while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost.
Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
Request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Storage Class Access Pattern
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 8349 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to evaluate lifecycle transition, retention lock, restore, and minimum-duration cost; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Storage Class Access Pattern.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Storage Class Access Pattern, keep Storage Class Access Pattern connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
